mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation above 2.5% (CPIAUCSL) triggers central bank rate hikes, increasing discount rates and reducing equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns exceed 1.5% in 70% of instances where CPIAUCSL > 2.5%, or Rank IC > 0.05 in inflationary regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in GDP (GDPC1) and residential construction (HOUST) signals robust economic demand, driving corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: 20-day SPX returns are negative in 60%+ of periods where both GDPC1 and HOUST exceed thresholds, or interaction term coefficient < 0.1 in regression.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in industrial production (INDPRO) indicates supply chain disruptions, increasing corporate costs and reducing earnings expectations, causing negative S&P 500 returns over 5 days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns are positive in 70%+ of instances with INDPRO declines > 3%, or Rank IC < 0.02 in supply disruption regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth (PAYEMS) above 150k signals labor market tightness, increasing wage pressures and corporate margins, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 60 days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns are negative in 50%+ of periods with PAYEMS > 150k, or interaction with unemployment rate reverses direction.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in real sales (RSAFS) combined with rising unemployment (UNRATE) indicates weakening consumer demand, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are positive in 60%+ of instances where RSAFS declines and UNRATE rises, or interaction term coefficient > 0.15.

mechanism_narrative: A dovish monetary policy shift (FEDFUNDS decline) during high unemployment (UNRATE > 5%) stimulates economic activity, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 5 days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns are negative in 50%+ of instances with FEDFUNDS < 0.5% and UNRATE > 5%, or interaction term coefficient < 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: A surge in industrial production (INDPRO) during low oil prices (CPIAUCSL < 2%) indicates strong supply-side dynamics, boosting corporate profits and generating positive S&P 500 returns over 60 days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns are negative in 50%+ of instances with INDPRO growth and CPIAUCSL < 2%, or interaction term coefficient < 0.1.
